partnership_policy_framework.governing_principles: Islamic principles of Amanah, Adl, Taraddi, and transparent business conduct

policy_001_partnership_governance.goal: Establish clear governance structures for all strategic partnerships
policy_statements: Every partnership ≥ $1M annual value shall have a dedicated Governance Council with representatives from both parties
Governance Council shall meet quarterly minimum to review: KPIs, issues, opportunities, financial performance
Escalation paths defined: Partner issues → Partner Manager → Director → CFO → Board
All partnership disputes first escalated to Islamic mediation (Tahkim), then arbitration if unresolved
policy_001_partnership_governance.enforcement: Non-compliance with governance meetings may trigger contract review/termination

policy_002_financial_terms.goal: Define transparent financial arrangements aligned with Islamic principles
policy_statements: All revenue sharing models must comply with Islamic Mudharabah (venture/profit-sharing) or Musharaka (partnership) models, NOT interest-based (Riba)
Revenue share models: Tier 1 partners 40% of net profit, Tier 2 partners 25%, Tier 3 partners 15%
All payments settled monthly within 45 days of invoice. Late payments accrue 2% monthly penalty (equivalent to Islamic penalty, NOT interest)
Zakat is calculated and paid by Sheikha on behalf of shared proceeds (2.5% of profits after operational costs)
All financial data encrypted, audited quarterly, and subject to both secular audit and Sharia board review
policy_002_financial_terms.enforcement: Financial terms are non-negotiable. Deviations require Board approval. Non-payment triggers default provisions.

policy_003_data_handling.goal: Protect user data and ensure compliance with privacy laws and Islamic principles
key_requirements: User data is NOT owned by Sheikha or partners. Users own their own data.
Data shared with partners ONLY with explicit user consent. Default is 'NO sharing'.
User can withdraw consent anytime. Data must be deleted within 30 days.
Data encrypted end-to-end. Partners cannot decrypt without explicit key from user.
Data location: Must reside in Saudi Arabia or GCC. NO transfer to Western servers.
Data retention: Transaction data kept 7 years per regulatory requirement, then deleted securely.
Partner access logged with timestamp, duration, and action. Logging required for audit.
policy_003_data_handling.islamic_basis: Qaul (Speech): Privacy of conversations. Hadith: 'None of you believes until they wish for their brother what they wish for themselves'
policy_003_data_handling.enforcement: Data breach consequence: Immediate contract suspension, financial penalty, possible termination

policy_004_intellectual_property.goal: Protect IP ownership while enabling collaboration
policy_statements: Pre-existing IP: Each party retains ownership of their pre-existing IP (code, algorithms, patents, trademarks)
New IP created jointly: Ownership determined by agreement. Default: Sheikha owns 50%, Partner owns 50%, with non-exclusive rights to both
Licensing model: Partners may license Sheikha IP (e.g., APIs) under clear terms, paid licenses, audit rights reserved
AI models: Sheikha-built models remain Sheikha property. Partner contributions to training data negotiable per agreement.
Source code: Partners' source code is never shared with Sheikha. Sheikha provides compiled API access only.
Patent cooperation: Joint patents (rare) require both parties' written consent to license, enforce, or abandon
policy_004_intellectual_property.enforcement: IP disputes resolved by arbitration. Injunctive relief available for unauthorized IP use.

policy_005_security_compliance.goal: Ensure all partners meet enterprise-grade security and compliance requirements
requirements: Mandatory security certifications: ISO 27001 (information security) for Tier 1 partners, SOC 2 Type II for Tier 2 partners
API security: OAuth 2.0, JWT tokens, TLS 1.3 encryption, rate limiting, DDoS protection
Data security: AES-256 encryption at-rest, TLS encryption in-transit, annual penetration testing
Compliance audits: Sheikha-initiated audits 1-2 times per year. Partner bears audit cost if major findings.
Incident response: Partner must report security incidents within 24 hours. Prolonged non-disclosure is material breach.
Vulnerability management: Partners must patch security vulnerabilities within SLA (Critical: 24 hours, High: 7 days, Medium: 30 days)
policy_005_security_compliance.enforcement: Non-compliance may trigger: (1) Probation period, (2) Data access restrictions, (3) Contract termination

policy_006_performance_slas.goal: Define performance expectations and ensure service quality

standard_slas.penalty: 1% of monthly fees credit per 0.1% below target

standard_slas.measurement: Synthetic monitoring from multiple geographic regions
standard_slas.penalty: 0.5% of monthly fees credit if SLA breached
Escalation to technical escalation path, possible service suspension

standard_slas.target: Critical: 1 hour, High: 4 hours, Medium: 24 hours
standard_slas.measurement: Ticket tracking system with timestamp logs

policy_006_performance_slas.enforcement: SLA breaches tracked. 3+ consecutive breaches in quarter triggers contract review.

policy_007_conflict_resolution.goal: Resolve disputes fairly and efficiently using Islamic principles first

escalation_model.process: Direct discussion between team leads, documented in writing

escalation_model.resolution: Signed agreement or escalation to next level

escalation_model.process: Mutually-agreed Islamic mediator (Qadi or scholar), conducted in Arabic
escalation_model.resolution: Binding mediation decision (Sulh), documented in Arabic & English

escalation_model.process: CEO-to-CEO or Board-level discussion, final attempt at mutual resolution
escalation_model.resolution: Binding settlement or proceed to arbitration

escalation_model.process: Binding arbitration under Saudi Arbitration Law or GCC Arbitration Center
escalation_model.arbitrator: Single for < $2M disputes, Three-arbitrator panel for ≥ $2M

policy_007_conflict_resolution.islamic_principle: Tahkim (Islamic arbitration) preferred over litigation. 'The best of you are those who are best to their families, and I am the best among you to my family.' (Tirmidhi) - implies preference for negotiated settlement

policy_008_termination_exit.goal: Provide clear, fair exit mechanisms while protecting both parties

termination_types.reason: Either party may terminate without cause
termination_types.obligation: Providing party pays transition costs for data transfer, API decommissioning, customer notification

termination_types.notice_period: Immediate or 30 days for cure period
causes: Material breach of agreement not cured within 30 days
Insolvency, bankruptcy, or financial distress of partner
Security breach or unresolved compliance violation
Violation of Islamic financing or Sharia principles
termination_types.remedy: Breaching party liable for damages, transition costs, and contract penalties

exit_obligations: Data transition: Partner must migrate/delete all Sheikha-related data within 30 days
API decommissioning: APIs sunset over 90-day period with clear migration path for customers
Customer communication: Joint statement to customers, alternative solutions provided
Financial settlement: Final invoices, profit distribution, Zakat settlement within 45 days
Confidentiality survive: Confidentiality obligations continue for 5 years post-termination
IP cleanup: Return or destroy Confidential Information, certify in writing
policy_008_termination_exit.enforcement: Exit non-compliance may result in legal action and damages claim

policy_009_ethical_conduct.goal: Maintain highest ethical standards aligned with Islamic principles
requirements: No bribery, kickbacks, or indirect payments for unfair advantage
No discrimination based on religion, ethnicity, gender (unless business-critical)
No false claims or misrepresentation in marketing/sales
Conflict of interest disclosure: Partners must disclose any competing commitments
Whistleblower protection: Employees reporting violations protected from retaliation
No forced labor, child labor, or human trafficking in partner's operations
Compliance with Saudi KACC (anti-corruption) law and UNCAC
policy_009_ethical_conduct.islamic_basis: Hadith: 'The best of transaction is truthfulness... The worst of you in Allah's sight on the Day of Judgment will be the liars.' (Tirmidhi)
policy_009_ethical_conduct.enforcement: Violations trigger: (1) Investigation, (2) Corrective action plan, (3) Possible termination

policy_010_sustainability_social.goal: Advance positive social and environmental impact
requirements: Environmental: Reduce carbon footprint, use renewable energy where possible, sustainable supply chain
Social: Contribute to Saudi Vision 2030 goals, employ Saudi nationals, invest in local communities
Data ethics: Fair AI algorithms (no bias), transparent decision-making, user consent for data use
Zakat compliance: Zakat calculated and paid on shared profits (2.5% of profits after costs)
Charitable giving: Partners encouraged to contribute 1% of profits to Islamic charitable causes
policy_010_sustainability_social.measurement: Annual sustainability report required from each Tier 1 partner, optional for lower tiers

term_and_renewal.renewal_option: Automatic renewal for 2-year periods unless either party provides 6 months' notice of non-renewal
term_and_renewal.extension: May be extended indefinitely if both parties consent and performance remains strong

term_conditions.requirement: Partner must maintain ≥ 95% of agreed KPIs over each quarter. Quarterly performance reports required.
Partner must maintain: (A) General liability insurance ($5M minimum), (B) Cyber liability insurance ($10M), (C) Indemnify Sheikha against third-party IP infringement claims
Partner must: (A) Maintain all required licenses/certifications, (B) Comply with sanctions lists, (C) Not conduct business with blocked parties or high-risk jurisdictions
Monthly reports: financial performance, KPIs, issues, security updates. Annual audits by independent 3rd party.

Partner must not: (A) Use Islamic branding for non-compliant products, (B) Engage in Riba-based (interest) products, (C) Support any form of prohibited (Haram) activities. Sharia council review annually.

consequence_1.breach_type: Minor Policy Violation (e.g., late report submission)
consequence_1.consequence: Written warning, 30-day cure period, no financial penalty

consequence_2.breach_type: Material Breach (e.g., 3+ consecutive SLA misses, security incident)
consequence_2.consequence: Probation period (60-90 days), corrective action plan required, possible revenue share reduction, 3rd-party audit at breaching party's cost

consequence_3.breach_type: Serious Breach (e.g., data privacy violation, Riba engagement, corruption)
consequence_3.consequence: Immediate suspension of partnership activities, financial penalties (5-10% of annual fees), escalation to arbitration, possible termination

consequence_4.breach_type: Fundamental Breach (e.g., major security breach, fraud, Sharia violation)
consequence_4.consequence: Immediate contract termination, all revenue forfeited, damages claim, possible referral to authorities

tier_1_terms.governance: Joint Board seat recommended, monthly steering committee
tier_1_terms.investment: Sheikha may invest in partner company equity (minority stake, <30%)
tier_1_terms.exclusivity: Partners may have exclusive geographic or product exclusivity (negotiated per deal)
tier_1_terms.support_level: 24/7 dedicated support team, SLA guarantees, quarterly business reviews with executive teams

tier_2_terms.governance: Quarterly partner reviews, dedicated account manager
tier_2_terms.investment: Sheikha may co-market, provide technical support, training
tier_2_terms.support_level: Business hours support, dedicated Slack/Teams channel, monthly check-ins

tier_3_terms.revenue_share: 15% of net profit or flat fee (negotiated)
tier_3_terms.support_level: Community forums, online documentation, email support (48-hour SLA)

automation_1.description: Key agreement terms (financial thresholds, SLA metrics, termination triggers) encoded as smart contracts on blockchain with automatic enforcement: e.g., if uptime < 99.8%, funds automatically withheld and held in escrow for dispute resolution
automation_1.benefit: Removes need for constant manual monitoring, ensures objective enforcement, reduces disputes

automation_2.description: Real-time dashboards feeding from partner APIs, cloud monitoring, and financial systems. KPIs auto-calculated and flagged if below threshold. Alerts sent to relevant stakeholders.
automation_2.benefit: Early warning system, transparency, objective performance visibility

automation_3.description: Monthly automated compliance checks: Is partner still certified ISO 27001? Are they on OFAC sanctions list? Do they have required insurance? Automated reports generated.

automation_4.description: 90 days before NDA expiration, automated notification to both parties. Renewal terms pre-filled. E-signature workflow auto-triggered. New NDA effective immediately upon e-signature.
automation_4.benefit: No lapse in confidentiality protection, reduced administrative burden

automation_5.description: Monthly financial data feeds directly from partner systems into Sheikha financial system. Revenue calculated automatically, Zakat (2.5%) calculated, settled within 45 days. Blockchain receipt retained.
automation_5.benefit: Transparent, rapid payment, Islamic compliance automated, audit trail permanent

islamic_compliance_certification.certification_standard: All partnership policies comply with Islamic Sharia principles
islamic_compliance_certification.certified_by: Dr. [Sharia Scholar Name], Head of Sheikha Sharia Board
islamic_principles_confirmed: ✓ No Riba (interest) - Mudharabah/Musharaka revenue models only
✓ No Gharar (deception) - Full transparency in all terms
✓ Taraddi (mutual consent) - All terms negotiated fairly
✓ Al-Amanah (trust) - Confidentiality and data security upheld
✓ Al-Adl (justice) - Fair treatment, transparent dispute resolution
✓ Zakat compliance - 2.5% calculated and paid on profits
